generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "The permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission system receiving power from the motor and reducing speed while increasing torque"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted encoder measuring motor angular position for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind gearbox measuring gearbox output angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

instance TorqueEstimationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor deriving torque estimates from motor current (I_M) using robot-specific dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueEstimationData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance TorqueEstimationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueEstimationSensor
]

instance MQTTStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentData, MotorPositionData, TorqueEstimationData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance IdealSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink simulation of ideal system behavior without friction for the first robot axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing LuGre friction characteristics in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black-box data-driven friction model derived from live sensor data in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor output angle (r_M) with friction effects subtracted from ideal model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealSystemModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel
]

instance GearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearbox output angle (r_G) derived from motor output with friction effects"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorOutputData
]

instance SystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated system output angle (r_out) accounting for all friction components"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxOutputData
]

instance MotorCurrentTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time motor current data stream (I_M) transmitted via MQTT from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStream
]

instance MotorPositionTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time motor position data trace (r_M) derived from motor encoder measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxPositionTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time gearbox position data trace (r_G) derived from secondary encoder measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionData
]

instance TorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time torque data trace (τ_M, τ_G) derived from motor current and dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueEstimationData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tools used for creating structural (BDD/IBD) and behavioral models of the first robot axis"
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform for executable friction simulations and model integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance MQTTSensorProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data streaming from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance CustomDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard for displaying data traces and model outputs"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying friction losses (static, kinetic, viscous) in real-time for control algorithm adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling continuous updates to friction models based on live data for motion accuracy optimization"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelUpdateAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring dashboard for comparing simulated and measured data"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction, MaintenanceTriggerAction
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling real-time data acquisition and transmission from robot sensors via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of friction losses (static, kinetic, viscous) by comparing simulated and measured data traces"
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic continuous updates to friction models based on live sensor data for dynamic adaptation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual parameter adjustment by operators to compensate for detected friction deviations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic maintenance trigger based on wear detection from friction analysis deviations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTraceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic understanding of data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) with metadata linking to their sources"
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator,
                 MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, TorqueEstimationSensor,
                 IdealSystemModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                 StribeckFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel,
                 BlackBoxFrictionModel
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and dynamic**, with:
   - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, angle) streamed at **real-time intervals** (not explicitly quantified but implied to be high-frequency for friction analysis).
   - **Model updates**: Continuous refinement of friction models during operation via **live data integration** and **multi-physics-aware identification** (combining white-box and black-box models).
   - **Dashboard synchronization**: Data and model outputs are updated in real-time to support **continuous monitoring** of friction-induced inaccuracies."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **single-axis-centric** for the first robot axis, with:
   - **Core components**:
     - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: Passive data aggregation and semantic linking of physical sensor data with simulation models (Fig. 1 reference model).
     - **Processing components**: Simulation models (Motor, Gearwheel, Gearbox) in Simulink, consuming input data traces (e.g., I_M) and producing output traces (e.g., ˆr_M).
   - **Orchestration**: The DS is designed as a **standalone system** for the first axis, with potential for integration into a broader digital twin framework (e.g., Asset Administration Shells, AAS) for multi-axis coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates primarily during the **operated stage** of the robot’s lifecycle, with a focus on:
   - **As-operated representation**: Capturing real-time friction behavior and deviations from idealized models during actual machining tasks.
   - **Scope**: The digital shadow is created to **understand friction loss** in the first axis for improving motion accuracy during operation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are addressed through:
   - **Verification/Validation**:
     - **Live data comparison**: Simulated vs. measured data traces (e.g., r_M, r_G) to validate friction models.
     - **Sensor redundancy**: Use of both motor and secondary encoders to infer gearbox torque/velocity and reduce positional uncertainty.
   - **Uncertainty Handling**:
     - **Model parameters**: Use of manufacturer-provided robot-specific parameters (e.g., motor dynamics) to ensure fidelity.
     - **Friction models**: Integration of multiple friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) to capture diverse effects (load, temperature, wear).
   - **Traceability**: Metadata links (e.g., Fig. 5) ensure transparency in data origin and model computation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) involves:
   - **Data Transmission**: **MQTT protocol** for real-time sensor data streaming from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to the DT.
   - **Network Architecture**: Implied **cyber-physical system (CPS) integration**, where sensor data and model outputs are exchanged between the robot’s hardware and DT software environment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is hosted/deployed as:
   - **Software Environment**: Embedded within the existing robot control infrastructure (SINUMERIK 840D) and Simulink-based simulations.
   - **Real-Time Processing**: Data and model computations occur in **parallel to robot operation**, ensuring low latency for friction analysis."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin leverages the following standards:
   - **Modeling**: **SysML** for structural/behavioral modeling (BDD/IBD).
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model**: Adherence to the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** from Michael et al. [15], including:
     - Purpose-driven design (e.g., friction analysis).
     - Data trace aggregation (e.g., Fig. 5 UML diagram).
     - Metadata for traceability (e.g., source channels).
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied integration potential for broader industrial IoT (IIoT) ecosystems."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The system includes implicit safety considerations:
   - **Data Integrity**: MQTT protocol ensures reliable transmission of sensor data to the DT.
   - **Operational Safety**: The digital shadow is designed for **non-interactive monitoring** (passive data flow), avoiding autonomous control actions that could risk human safety.
   - **No Remote Execution**: The DT does not generate commands to the robot (virtual-to-physical interaction is absent), mitigating unintended risks."
]
